varieties.name: Standard Tamarind
varieties.origin: Africa, cultivated globally for millennia
varieties.traits: Large tree 15-25m (can reach 30m); evergreen with pinnate leaves; brown pods 10-15cm long
varieties.yield_notes: Mature trees 150-225kg annually; seedlings fruit at 6-8 years, grafts at 3-4 years
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery (Queensland), Ladybird Nursery (Australia), various Queensland suppliers

varieties.name: Thai Sweet Tamarind
varieties.origin: Thailand (Phetchabun province primary source)
varieties.traits: Similar growth to standard; larger, plumper pods; bred specifically for fresh consumption
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding; 330-500 pods per mature tree annually
varieties.availability: Specialty tropical fruit nurseries, may require import permits

varieties.name: Sithong (Golden Sweet)
varieties.origin: Thailand (Phetchabun geographical indication variety)
varieties.traits: Curved pods; even brown skin; thick flesh
varieties.availability: Specialty importers, grafted material may be available through tropical fruit networks

varieties.name: Kunthee (Khandee)
varieties.origin: Thailand (straight pod variety)
varieties.traits: Straight fruit form; distinctive shape
varieties.availability: Limited; through specialty sources

varieties.name: Pragaithong
varieties.origin: Thailand (straight pod type)
varieties.traits: Straight pods; golden characteristics
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Native Tamarind (Diploglottis australis) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to Queensland and NSW subtropical rainforests (Mackay to Illawarra)
varieties.traits: Slender tree to 30m in rainforest, 8-10m in gardens; large pinnate leaves up to 135cm; rusty-brown new growth; evergreen
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific fruiting; impressive quantities when mature
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, Tucker Bush, Brisbane native plant nurseries, widely available throughout Queensland

varieties.name: Small-leaved Tamarind (Diploglottis campbellii) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to Queensland and northern NSW (Bonogin Creek area and border ranges)
varieties.traits: Small, dense tree; smaller leaves than D. australis; critically endangered in wild
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific fruiter; many kilos per harvest possible
varieties.availability: Tucker Bush, specialized native plant nurseries, conservation nurseries

varieties.name: Kerala Red Tamarind 🏆
varieties.origin: Kerala, indigenous selection, cultivated for centuries

varieties.name: Kerala Brown Tamarind 🏆
varieties.origin: Kerala, traditional selection
varieties.traits: Larger tree; longer pods; bigger seeds than red type; brown variety common in South India
varieties.availability: Widely available Kerala nurseries, common roadside plantings

varieties.name: PKM-1 (Periyakulam-1)
varieties.origin: Horticultural College and Research Institute, Periyakulam, Tamil Nadu (Released 1992)
varieties.traits: Clonal selection; early bearer; purple pigmentation in terminal buds; dark brown stems; fruits borne in clusters of 4-5; slightly curved pods with characteristic semicircle shape
varieties.yield_notes: 263kg pods per tree (9-year-old tree); 25-26 tonnes per hectare at 10m x 10m spacing; grafts fruit at 3rd year, commercial yield from 5th year
varieties.availability: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University, Kerala agricultural nurseries, Indian horticultural institutes

varieties.name: Urigam 🏆
varieties.origin: Superior local genotype near Urigam, Thenkanikottai, Dharmapuri district, Tamil Nadu; progeny of 200+ year old tree
varieties.traits: Short branching like an umbrella; characteristic flat inward 'C' shaped curve; late flowering in March; lengthy, fleshy pods averaging 30cm length x 6.25cm width; 150-200g per fruit
varieties.yield_notes: Mother tree yields 2 tonnes of fruit per year; grafts begin at 4th year; seedlings at 6-8 years; 2-3 pods per bunch
varieties.availability: Tamil Nadu Department of Horticulture, Kerala horticultural stations, Karur region Tamil Nadu

varieties.name: Prathisthan (Prathithan)
varieties.origin: Fruit Research Station, Aurangabad, Maharashtra
varieties.traits: High yielder; regular bearer; large fruits (45 fruits per kg)
varieties.yield_notes: 300kg per tree (10-year-old); 6-10 quintal fruits per tree for mature specimens
varieties.availability: Maharashtra agricultural stations, available through Kerala horticultural departments

varieties.name: Yogeshwari (Yogeswari)
varieties.origin: Taluk Seed Farm, Ambajogai, Beed district, Maharashtra; Forest Department, Karnataka selection
varieties.traits: Large fruits; high-yielding variety
varieties.availability: Karnataka Forest Department, Maharashtra seed farms, Kerala horticultural centers

varieties.name: DTS-1 (Dharwad Tamarind Selection-1)
varieties.origin: University of Agricultural Sciences, Dharwad, College of Horticulture, Arabhavi, Karnataka
varieties.traits: Sweet red type; straight pods with semi-curved shape; 23.6cm length x 3cm width; 19.5g weight; late variety
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding regular variety; grafted plants start yielding in 4-5 years; 3kg at 5th year average
varieties.availability: Karnataka agricultural universities, horticultural research stations

varieties.name: DTS-2 (Dharwad Tamarind Selection-2)
varieties.origin: University of Agricultural Sciences, Dharwad, Karnataka
varieties.traits: Straight pods with semi-curved shape; 17.6cm length x 2.6cm width; 18g weight; early variety
varieties.availability: Karnataka agricultural sources

varieties.name: Goma Prateek
varieties.origin: Central Institute for Arid Horticulture (CIAH), India
varieties.traits: Short juvenile period (3-4 years); early bearer; promising variety for western India

varieties.name: Bangalore Tamarind 🏆
varieties.origin: Bangalore region, Karnataka; local seedling type
varieties.traits: Traditional local variety; brown sour type classification
varieties.yield_notes: Variable as seedling origin; 150-225kg mature tree

varieties.name: Tumkur Tamarind 🏆
varieties.origin: Tumkur district, Karnataka; traditional local type
varieties.traits: Local seedling selection; regional variety
varieties.availability: Tumkur region Karnataka, local selections

varieties.name: Hosur Tamarind 🏆
varieties.origin: Hosur, Tamil Nadu (border region near Karnataka and Kerala)
varieties.traits: Local traditional variety; brown sour type
varieties.availability: Hosur region Tamil Nadu

varieties.name: Krishnagiri Tamarind 🏆
varieties.origin: Krishnagiri district, Tamil Nadu
varieties.traits: Local traditional type; regional selection
varieties.availability: Krishnagiri district sources

varieties.name: Natham Tamarind 🏆
varieties.origin: Natham, Tamil Nadu
varieties.traits: Traditional local variety
varieties.availability: Natham region Tamil Nadu

varieties.name: Nagarkoil Tamarind 🏆
varieties.origin: Nagercoil (Nagarkoil), Tamil Nadu (southernmost tip, near Kerala border)
varieties.traits: Coastal variety; traditional type
varieties.availability: Nagercoil region, southern Tamil Nadu and Kerala border

varieties.name: Villupuram Tamarind 🏆
varieties.origin: Villupuram district, Tamil Nadu
varieties.traits: Local traditional variety
varieties.availability: Villupuram district sources

varieties.name: Cumbum Selection 🏆
varieties.origin: Cumbum, Madurai district, Tamil Nadu (near Kerala border)
varieties.traits: Local selection from Cumbum Valley; traditional type
varieties.availability: Cumbum Valley Tamil Nadu, Madurai district

varieties.name: Karur Urigam Tamarind 🏆
varieties.origin: Karur, Tamil Nadu; related to Urigam variety
varieties.traits: Local selection; similar characteristics to Urigam type
varieties.availability: Karur region Tamil Nadu

varieties.name: Kangadevanapalli Tamarind 🏆
varieties.origin: Kangadevanapalli, Tamil Nadu; local selection
varieties.traits: Traditional local variety
varieties.availability: Local sources Tamil Nadu

varieties.name: Dever Ullimangalam Tamarind 🏆
varieties.origin: Ullimangalam, Tamil Nadu; local selection
varieties.traits: Traditional local type
varieties.availability: Local sources Tamil Nadu

varieties.name: Rahuri Selection 🏆
varieties.origin: Rahuri, Maharashtra; local selection
varieties.traits: Maharashtra traditional variety
varieties.availability: Rahuri Maharashtra, agricultural stations

varieties.name: Ranchi Tamarind 🏆
varieties.origin: Ranchi region, Jharkhand/Eastern India
varieties.traits: Eastern India variety; brown sour type
varieties.availability: Eastern India sources

varieties.name: Lakshamana
varieties.origin: Nandihalli village, Tumkur district, Karnataka; ICAR-Indian Institute of Horticultural Research selection (2016-2020)
varieties.traits: Farmer's selection through participatory breeding; broad pods; superior characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: 251.4kg per tree mean annual yield (4-year average); significantly better than local types (165kg)
varieties.availability: ICAR-Indian Institute of Horticultural Research, Bengaluru; Karnataka horticultural stations

varieties.name: Jagdish
varieties.origin: Maharashtra
varieties.traits: Sweet-sour balanced variety; high yielding
varieties.availability: Maharashtra agricultural sources

varieties.name: Raktichinch
varieties.origin: India (name means 'blood tamarind')
varieties.traits: Distinctive red pulp variety
varieties.availability: Specialized Indian horticultural sources; high demand variety

varieties.name: Malabar Tamarind / Kudampuli (Garcinia cambogia) 🌏
varieties.origin: Endemic to Western Ghats; native to Kerala coastal and Karnataka regions
varieties.traits: Small to medium tree with rounded crown; horizontal branches; dark green shining leaves 2-7cm; yellow or red fruit when ripe; resembles small pumpkin 4-5cm diameter with 6-8 longitudinal grooves
varieties.availability: Greens of Kerala nursery, Kerala native plant suppliers, Western Ghats region nurseries, widely cultivated in Kerala
